decision.recommended_next_action: Finalize this branch and, if needed later, promote the monotonic-dwell vLLM patch plus real-vLLM benchmark harness into an upstreamable integration hardening task.
decision.stop_reason: The high-concurrency real-vLLM waiting-request stall was root-caused to an epoch-vs-monotonic dwell clock mismatch and fixed; KV and dwell stress reruns completed with zero timeouts and parsed Prometheus speculative metrics.

recommended_next_action: Finalize this branch and, if needed later, promote the monotonic-dwell vLLM patch plus real-vLLM benchmark harness into an upstreamable integration hardening task.
run_notes_tail: - Patched `[redacted-local-vllm-checkout]/vllm/v1/request.py`, `scripts/install_vllm_spec_governor_patch.py`, and `patches/vllm_spec_governor_contract.patch` so the dwell deadline uses `time.monotonic() + bounded_dwell`.
- Updated `tests/test_vllm_spec_governor_patch_contract.py` to lock the monotonic-clock invariant.
- Hardened `scripts/benchmark_openai_governor_endpoint.py`:
- timeout/URL errors now become per-request error rows instead of aborting the whole benchmark;
- real-vLLM `/v1` base URLs query root `/metrics` first and parse speculative Prometheus counters;
- added `--prompt-char-limit` for small `max_model_len` real-vLLM stress runs so validation errors do not obscure scheduler behavior.
### Verification and benchmark artifacts

- Contract regression passed after the dwell-clock fix: `results/vllm_spec_governor_patch_contract_test_after_dwell_clock_fix.log` and final rerun `results/vllm_spec_governor_patch_contract_test_final.log`.
- Py-compile passed for project scripts/modules, the regression test, and patched vLLM scheduler/request files: `results/project_pycompile_final.log`.

- Real patched vLLM server was launched from `/tmp/enoch_vllm_services` with Torch `2.11.0+cu130`, CUDA 13 ptxas, V1 engine, ngram speculation, and Triton attention. Helper server was stopped before yielding.
#### KV/concurrency stress after fix
Artifact: `results/real_vllm_torch211_vllm_xargs_kv_stress_dwellfix_promptcap`
- Config: 32 requests, client concurrency 8, `vllm_xargs`, prompt cap 512 chars, model `smollm2-135m`.
- Fixed policy: 32/32 success, p95 `1052.83 ms`, throughput `1395.075 tok/s`.
- Governor policy: 32/32 success, p95 `742.215 ms`, throughput `1792.329 tok/s`, mean dwell `0.6754 ms`, mean requested spec cap `5.938`.
- Parsed endpoint metrics at completion: running `0`, waiting `0`, KV pressure `0.0`, speculative accepted `1601`, drafted `2338`.
#### Dwell stress after fix
Artifact: `results/real_vllm_torch211_vllm_xargs_dwell_stress_dwellfix_promptcap`
- Config: 96 requests, client concurrency 16, `vllm_xargs`, prompt cap 512 chars, model `smollm2-135m`.
- Fixed policy: 96/96 success, p95 `784.797 ms`, throughput `3047.964 tok/s`.
- Governor policy: 96/96 success, p95 `792.178 ms`, throughput `2996.574 tok/s`, mean dwell `0.9636 ms`, mean requested spec cap `6.229`.
- Deltas: p95 `+0.94%`, throughput `-1.686%`.
- Parsed endpoint metrics at completion: running `0`, waiting `0`, KV pressure `0.0`, speculative accepted `7745`, drafted `10474`.
### Interpretation update

The remaining high-concurrency stall was a patch bug, not a GB10/Torch/Triton backend deadlock: positive dwell deadlines were measured on the wrong clock. After switching dwell deadlines to `time.monotonic()`, the same real-vLLM integration path completes both KV/concurrency and dwell stress runs with no waiting-request residue and no client timeouts. The branch now has strong model-backed evidence that the governor can control live speculative caps and bounded dwell through OpenAI-compatible `vllm_xargs` under concurrent real vLLM load. The generic benchmark `success_rule_met` remains false only because stock vLLM does not return per-request waste/KV fields, so waste and peak-KV deltas stay zero even though Prometheus speculative counters are parsed successfully.

- Patched `[redacted-local-vllm-checkout]/vllm/v1/request.py`, `scripts/install_vllm_spec_governor_patch.py`, and `patches/vllm_spec_governor_contract.patch` so the dwell deadline uses `time.monotonic() + bounded_dwell`.
- Updated `tests/test_vllm_spec_governor_patch_contract.py` to lock the monotonic-clock invariant.
- Hardened `scripts/benchmark_openai_governor_endpoint.py`:
- timeout/URL errors now become per-request error rows instead of aborting the whole benchmark;
- real-vLLM `/v1` base URLs query root `/metrics` first and parse speculative Prometheus counters;
- added `--prompt-char-limit` for small `max_model_len` real-vLLM stress runs so validation errors do not obscure scheduler behavior.
### Verification and benchmark artifacts

- Contract regression passed after the dwell-clock fix: `results/vllm_spec_governor_patch_contract_test_after_dwell_clock_fix.log` and final rerun `results/vllm_spec_governor_patch_contract_test_final.log`.
- Py-compile passed for project scripts/modules, the regression test, and patched vLLM scheduler/request files: `results/project_pycompile_final.log`.

- Real patched vLLM server was launched from `/tmp/enoch_vllm_services` with Torch `2.11.0+cu130`, CUDA 13 ptxas, V1 engine, ngram speculation, and Triton attention. Helper server was stopped before yielding.
#### KV/concurrency stress after fix
Artifact: `results/real_vllm_torch211_vllm_xargs_kv_stress_dwellfix_promptcap`
- Config: 32 requests, client concurrency 8, `vllm_xargs`, prompt cap 512 chars, model `smollm2-135m`.
- Fixed policy: 32/32 success, p95 `1052.83 ms`, throughput `1395.075 tok/s`.
- Governor policy: 32/32 success, p95 `742.215 ms`, throughput `1792.329 tok/s`, mean dwell `0.6754 ms`, mean requested spec cap `5.938`.
- Parsed endpoint metrics at completion: running `0`, waiting `0`, KV pressure `0.0`, speculative accepted `1601`, drafted `2338`.
#### Dwell stress after fix
Artifact: `results/real_vllm_torch211_vllm_xargs_dwell_stress_dwellfix_promptcap`
- Config: 96 requests, client concurrency 16, `vllm_xargs`, prompt cap 512 chars, model `smollm2-135m`.
- Fixed policy: 96/96 success, p95 `784.797 ms`, throughput `3047.964 tok/s`.
- Governor policy: 96/96 success, p95 `792.178 ms`, throughput `2996.574 tok/s`, mean dwell `0.9636 ms`, mean requested spec cap `6.229`.
- Deltas: p95 `+0.94%`, throughput `-1.686%`.
- Parsed endpoint metrics at completion: running `0`, waiting `0`, KV pressure `0.0`, speculative accepted `7745`, drafted `10474`.
### Interpretation update

The remaining high-concurrency stall was a patch bug, not a GB10/Torch/Triton backend deadlock: positive dwell deadlines were measured on the wrong clock. After switching dwell deadlines to `time.monotonic()`, the same real-vLLM integration path completes both KV/concurrency and dwell stress runs with no waiting-request residue and no client timeouts. The branch now has strong model-backed evidence that the governor can control live speculative caps and bounded dwell through OpenAI-compatible `vllm_xargs` under concurrent real vLLM load. The generic benchmark `success_rule_met` remains false only because stock vLLM does not return per-request waste/KV fields, so waste and peak-KV deltas stay zero even though Prometheus speculative counters are parsed successfully.
